assets.name: Bitcoin

score_rationales.institutional: ETF flows, corporate treasuries, major custody support
score_rationales.regulatory: ETF approved, commodity classification in most jurisdictions
score_rationales.revenue: Bitcoin generates transaction fees (~$1-2M daily) paid to miners, but has no protocol-level revenue capture mechanism—fees go entirely to miners, not a treasury or token holders. As a store-of-value asset with fee_model: burn economics (fees consumed in mining rewards), revenue dimension is largely inapplicable; value accrues through scarcity and network effects rather than fee generation.
score_rationales.supply: Data-driven score. Fixed supply cap, 90%+ circulating, minimal inflation.
score_rationales.wyckoff: Manual override: Phase B→C

assets.note: Strong institutional adoption. Regulatory clarity. Wyckoff spring zone

assets.name: Uniswap

score_rationales.regulatory: SEC dropped Wells notice investigation in Feb 2025 with no enforcement action; Bitwise filed spot UNI ETF in Feb 2026; BlackRock partnership signals institutional comfort; class action dismissed in federal court. However, broader DeFi regulatory framework remains unsettled and ETF approval is uncertain.
score_rationales.revenue: Daily revenue: $256K (~$93.5M/year). TVL: $3.30B. Revenue/TVL ratio: 2.83%. Daily fees: $2.8M.
score_rationales.supply: UNI has a fixed 1B max supply with 70.7% circulating, and the UNIfication proposal burned 100M tokens (~10% of supply), transforming it into a deflationary asset with ongoing fee burns. However, high whale concentration (top 10 addresses hold ~50% of supply) and recent whale selling ahead of macro events partially offset these positives. The completed vesting schedule (2024) eliminates unlock pressure, and the new UNIVesting contract releases only 5M UNI quarterly for ecosystem growth.
